violations.text: Ham was observed held at 44 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC placed in another fridge. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/04/2025

Inspector observed several items in containers without proper covering in the the lowboy freezer unit in the kitchen area. PIC covered items. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the side of pizza oven (side next to fryer) have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and/or other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2025
The bar area is lacking a hand-washing sink. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2025
The hand-washing sinks in the bathrooms were lacking proper hand-washing signage. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/18/2024

Deli meat (ham) was observed held at 46 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC placed in walk-in cooler. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2025
The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2025
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2025

Thermometers are lacking in the refrigeration units. Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2025

The establishment is lacking a thermometer to test food temperatures. Food temperature measuring devices shall be provided and be readily accessible for checking cooking, cooling, reheating, and hot/cold holding temperatures of time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2025

The doors to the outside, located at the front of the building, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2025
Inspector observed several items in containers without proper covering in the lowboy freezer unit in the kitchen. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2025

Knives are stored between prep units and table between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the dishwasher. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2025

The following non-food contact surfaces have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and/or other debris: - table under flat top grill, - sides of pizza oven, - prep table shelves, - prep units. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2025
The door to the kitchen toilet room is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2025
The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2025

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 04/11/2024

Inspector observed handwashing signage lacking at the bar and bathroom handwashing sinks. A sign or poster that notifies FOOD EMPLOYEES to wash their hands shall be provided at all HANDWASHING SINKS. New Violation. Correct By: 04/18/2024

Various food product was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 04/11/2024

Knives were observed being stored in between prep units. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. New Violation. Correct By: 04/11/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the shelves in kitchen area have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 04/11/2024
Observed leak at pipes located at the handwashing sink in ware washing area. Systems must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/11/2024
